blocks.content: The grass was April-high and damp, the tree line the same dark shape against the same dark sky.
They stopped at the edge of it.
Not the full summer display — April was too early for that — but a few, here and there, blinking in and out of the dark.
She laughed, soft, and the laugh loosened something that had been tight in her chest for five days. He reached out and took her hand in the dark. She held it.
He reached into his jacket with his free hand and came out with his phone.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Because it's ours. Because I want to remember it the right way.
He raised the phone. The fireflies drifted.
She had been waiting for the right moment.
